assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable in at least one group entity before consolidation.
The question concerns the accounting treatment in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 requirements on eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements, including the specific foreign currency hedge accounting model for certain intragroup exposures.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IAS 21 accounting for foreign currency monetary items and intragroup monetary balances in consolidation, which is the non-hedge accounting model relevant to exchange differences.
